skill.name: project-creator
skill.description: Creates structured CCGG Business Operations project workspaces with CLAUDE.md guidance, README files, project index entries, dependency metadata, optional phase trackers, and validation checks.
skill.summary: Create CCGG project workspaces with templates, dependency tracking, and validation.
skill.author: Daron Vener

security_audit.summary: AI review found that most static findings are false positives from markdown examples, relative documentation paths, repository metadata, and ordinary words such as PARENT SYSTEM INTEGRATION. No evidence of malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, obfuscation, or network exfiltration was found. The remaining risk is medium because the skill intentionally writes local project files and its validation scripts construct paths from an unvalidated project name.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated Project Name Used In Local Paths
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The Python and Bash validators build project and index paths directly from the project name. A value containing path traversal segments could make the validators inspect files outside the intended CCGG incubator and index folders. The impact is limited because the validators mainly read files and print status, but the scripts do not enforce the hyphen-case constraint documented in SKILL.md.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The vulnerable path construction is directly visible in both validators. Confidence is not higher because the scripts are local validation helpers and do not print full file contents.

medium_findings.title: Intentional Filesystem Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE with legitimate purpose. The skill instructs the assistant to create project folders, write CLAUDE.md and README.md files, generate optional phase trackers, and add operations log entries. This is expected for a project scaffolding skill, but marketplace users need a warning because it changes local workspace files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file creation and logging workflow is clearly documented as intended behavior. It is a medium marketplace risk because the behavior is broad but not malicious.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged As Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Most external command hits are fenced markdown examples or usage snippets, not hidden code paths. The commands are visible validation and logging examples for the user to run manually.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation blocks and visible command examples. No concealed command execution or dynamic shell construction was found in those markdown sections.

low_findings.title: Sensitive And Crypto Keyword Matches Are Terminology Collisions
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The critical sensitive matches are caused by project terminology such as PARENT SYSTEM INTEGRATION, and weak crypto matches are caused by markdown filenames or ordinary documentation text. No Windows SAM database access or cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text shows business process terminology and template documentation. There is no evidence of credential database access or weak cryptographic algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Repository URL Flagged As Network Risk
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URL in SKILL.md is repository metadata, not code that performs a network request. No fetch, curl, webhook, or exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in frontmatter metadata only. No network-capable implementation was identified during contextual review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path Traversal Possible In Validator Inputs
dangerous_patterns.description: Project names are joined into filesystem paths without normalization, allow-list validation, or rejection of traversal segments. Add validation that accepts only the documented hyphen-case format before reading project files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path construction is direct and lacks validation in both script versions. Impact is constrained by local execution and mostly read-only validation behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Grep Pattern Built From Unvalidated User Input
dangerous_patterns.description: The Bash validator passes the project name directly to grep. Quoting prevents shell command injection, but a project name that begins with an option-like value or contains special regular expression syntax can alter validation behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The grep call is visible and uses unvalidated input as the pattern. Confidence is moderate because this is validation bypass or error risk, not direct code execution.

content.value_statement: Project setup can miss required documentation, index updates, dependency tracking, and operations logs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a repeatable CCGG project creation workflow.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of simple or complex project structures under Active Projects/_Incubator.
Generates CLAUDE.md content from simple and complex project templates.
Adds project index metadata with strategic alignment and dependency fields.
Creates optional multi-phase tracker content for projects that need phased delivery.
Documents operations log entries and completion summaries for new projects.
Provides Python and Bash validators that check required project files and sections.
limitations: The workflow is tailored to CCGG Business Operations naming and folder conventions.
Project creation is mostly manual; the planned create_project script is not implemented.
Validators check file presence and required text, not business quality or completeness.
Users should review generated files before relying on automatic logging or index updates.

use_cases.title: Launch a new incubator project
use_cases.description: Create the standard project folder, guidance file, README, index entry, and validation checklist for a new CCGG initiative.

use_cases.title: Coordinate complex project dependencies
use_cases.description: Capture upstream dependencies, downstream consumers, integration notes, and handoff expectations for multi-project work.

use_cases.title: Standardize Claude project context
use_cases.description: Generate consistent CLAUDE.md files so future Claude Code and Codex sessions understand project purpose, scope, and operating rules.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple project
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one self-contained project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the project-creator skill to create a simple CCGG incubator project named [project-name]. Ask me for purpose, deliverables, avatars, and success criteria before drafting files.

prompt_templates.title: Add dependency metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new CCGG project and help me identify blocking dependencies, downstream dependencies, and related parallel work before writing the project index entry.

prompt_templates.title: Build a phased project plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced setup for validation or rollout phases.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use project-creator to set up a multi-phase project. Suggest phase names, durations, completion criteria, reminders, and transition triggers based on my project purpose.

prompt_templates.title: Validate an existing project
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit of an already created project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my existing CCGG project against the project-creator validation checklist. Identify missing files, missing CLAUDE.md sections, unresolved placeholders, and index gaps.

output_examples.input: Create a simple research project for Dream 100 strategy.
output_examples.output: A proposed incubator project with a project name, purpose, deliverables, CLAUDE.md plan, README plan, index entry requirements, and validation checklist.

output_examples.input: Create a complex pricing strategy project with dependencies.
output_examples.output: A complex project setup that includes upstream dependencies, downstream consumers, coordination hub files, integration notes, and a completion summary.

output_examples.input: Check whether my new project is complete.
output_examples.output: A validation report showing required files, missing sections, unresolved placeholders, operations log status, and recommended fixes.

best_practices: Use hyphen-case project names and keep them short enough for folder and index paths.
Confirm dependencies explicitly, even when the project appears self-contained.
Run the Python validator after files are created and review warnings before marking setup complete.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for non-CCGG folder structures without adapting paths and templates.
Do not skip index or operations log updates when creating long-lived projects.
Do not leave template placeholders in generated project files.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically create every project file?
faq.answer: No. It mainly guides file creation from templates. The automated create_project script is documented as planned, not implemented.

faq.question: Can it create both simple and complex projects?
faq.answer: Yes. Simple projects use standard files. Complex projects add coordination guidance, dependency tracking, and integration sections.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it access the network?
faq.answer: No network behavior was found. The only URL observed is repository metadata in SKILL.md.

faq.question: Why does the audit mention filesystem risk?
faq.answer: The skill is designed to create and validate local project files, so filesystem access is part of its intended behavior.

faq.question: Should generated files be reviewed?
faq.answer: Yes. Review generated paths, dependencies, placeholders, and operations log entries before relying on the project setup.
